evidenceNeededDraft: Identify the specific applicable equipment/systems or workplace location involved.
Confirm whether physical or administrative safety controls for operational activity are in place and operational.
Review training records, inspection checklists, or maintenance documentation relevant to ppe.
Verify conformance with standard citations: 29 CFR 1910.132, 29 CFR 1910.133, 29 CFR 1910.134, 29 CFR 1910.135, 29 CFR 1910.136, 29 CFR 1910.138, 29 CFR 1910.95.
[Advisory Only] Qualified safety personnel must verify specific evidence requirements on-site.
correctiveActionPatternsDraft: Restrict access to or isolate any hazardous conditions related to hazard exposure immediately.
Inspect, repair, or replace any defective components of the affected equipment before returning to service.
Implement engineering, administrative, or PPE controls to minimize risks from hazard exposure.
Instruct workers on safe practices regarding ppe and verify procedural compliance.
Document corrective actions completed and ensure compliance with citations: 29 CFR 1910.132, 29 CFR 1910.133, 29 CFR 1910.134, 29 CFR 1910.135, 29 CFR 1910.136, 29 CFR 1910.138, 29 CFR 1910.95.
[Advisory Only] Corrective action details must be reviewed and approved by qualified safety managers or engineers.
applicabilityDraft: Applies to operations within jurisdiction: osha_general_industry.
Applies when task, activity, or condition involves equipment/tools or associated subsystems.
Applies when workers are exposed to work process risks during normal or maintenance operations.
[Advisory Only] Scope of applicability must be validated by a qualified inspector or safety professional.
